CONSTITUTIVE.definition: A definitional or identity relationship where the architectural feature literally is the mechanism. The evidence survives transfer nearly perfectly because the relationship is intrinsic. Example: window area constitutively determines daylight exposure at the retina—the laboratory measure (lux at the eye) and the architectural variable (window transmittance × sky luminance) are physically identical.

MECHANISM.definition: A completely traced causal pathway linking the architectural feature to the outcome through a known biological or physical mechanism. The pathway is decomposed link by link (e.g., window → daylight → retinal stimulation → serotonin synthesis → mood improvement), and each link is empirically or mechanistically supported.

EMPIRICAL_ASSOCIATION.definition: A robust, replicated empirical correlation between an architectural feature and an outcome, where the mechanism may be unknown or partially understood. The evidence establishes that variation in the feature correlates consistently with variation in the outcome, without requiring complete mechanistic decomposition.

FUNCTIONAL.definition: Evidence that an architectural feature produces a functional effect where the same function transfers across contexts, even though the underlying mechanism is unspecified or partially known. The feature reliably produces the effect in question, but the pathway is not fully traced.

CAPACITY.definition: Evidence that a system has the demonstrated capacity to produce an effect under controlled or highly favorable conditions, without direct evidence that the effect occurs reliably in typical real-world contexts. The feature can produce the effect; whether it will do so in situ depends on other enabling conditions not yet characterized.

ANALOGICAL.definition: Evidence drawn from structural analogy between the studied domain and the target domain, where the evidence comes from one field but is applied by analogy to another. The analogical relationship is systematic but not mechanistically grounded or empirically replicated in the target domain.

THEORY_DERIVED.definition: Evidence derived from prediction of a named theoretical framework that has not yet been empirically tested in the architectural domain. The theory is established in its home domain; the claim that the theory applies in architecture is speculative. Transfer depends on whether the theory's assumptions hold in the architectural context.
